news.title: Microsoft unveils Majorana 2, wants useful quantum computing by 2029
news.description: Microsoft has unveiled Majorana 2, a next-generation quantum chip it says brings practical quantum computing closer to reality. The company claims significantly improved qubit stability and a path toward commercially useful quantum systems by 2029.

news.title: Iran war: Even a peace deal won't fix energy crunch
news.description: The growing energy and supply chain crisis is unlikely to ease even if the US and Iran step back from the brink. Experts warn that reopening the Strait of Hormuz and repairing infrastructure could take months or years.

news.title: Nvidia CEO mounts charm push in South Korea with TV talk show, baseball appearances
news.description: While a celebrity in his own right, the charm push by the Taiwan-born 63-year-old highlights South Korea's critical position in the AI landscape. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix between them make about 70% of the memory needed for AI chips like Nvidia's.

news.title: TSMC boss betting big on AI growth
news.description: Taiwan's TSMC, the world's largest contract chipmaker, is confident in its growth over the next few years, thanks to robust demand for computing power and advanced semiconductors as it rides a relentless AI boom, its CEO said today.

stocks.ai_explanation: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) is a leading provider of advanced semiconductor manufacturing services. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) is a major player in the personal computer and graphics processing unit (GPU) market. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.
Applied Materials, Inc. (ASML) is a Dutch multinational technology company specializing in photolithography equipment and materials. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.
Intel Corporation (INTC) is a leading integrated circuit design and microprocessor manufacturer. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.
Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. (QCOM) is a leading provider of mobile broadband solutions. This is an educational summary, not financial advice.

disclaimer: Educational use only. Not financial advice.
